operationalSituations.description: Developer runs gofusa check during active development — tool findings inform safety-critical code changes
CI/CD pipeline runs gofusa as a gating check before release — tool exit code determines whether a build proceeds
Auditor reviews gofusa-generated evidence bundles to make certification decisions

hazards.description: False negative — gofusa fails to report a genuine safety rule violation
hazards.source: Incomplete rule coverage, rule logic error, or silent analysis failure

hazards.description: Wrong ASIL determination — gofusa computes an incorrect ASIL level from S/E/C inputs
hazards.source: ASIL lookup table error or off-by-one in DetermineASIL

hazards.description: Silent failure — gofusa exits 0 when analysis encountered errors
hazards.source: Unhandled error paths in CLI or engine returning nil instead of error

hazards.description: Evidence integrity violation — generated evidence bundles lack integrity verification, enabling undetected tampering
hazards.source: Missing SHA-256 hash in provenance or evidence bundle

hazards.description: Config silently disables safety checks — .fusa.json rule suppression masks active violations
hazards.source: Overly permissive rule disable without warning

safetyGoals.description: gofusa shall report every violation of activated safety rules with no false negatives
safetyGoals.safeState: Halt analysis and exit non-zero if rule engine cannot complete

safetyGoals.description: gofusa ASIL allocation shall match the standard's classification table exactly as configured
safetyGoals.safeState: Report configuration mismatch as ERROR finding

safetyGoals.description: gofusa shall exit non-zero when any ERROR-severity finding is generated or when analysis fails
gofusa evidence bundles shall include SHA-256 hashes for all generated artifacts
safetyGoals.safeState: Reject bundle if hash missing or mismatched

safetyGoals.description: gofusa shall emit a WARNING finding when a rule from a critical category is disabled in configuration
